summary: Package hijacks global console to exfiltrate log output to attacker-controlled Telegram bot on require()
details: On `require('8oo')`, index.js immediately loads 66o.js, which replaces the global `console` object with a Proxy. Every subsequent call to `console.log`, `console.error`, `console.dir`, etc. in the host process is intercepted and POSTed to a hardcoded Telegram Bot API endpoint (`https://api.telegram.org/bot989543891:.../sendMessage`) with hardcoded chat IDs, and mirrored to a hardcoded Firebase endpoint (`iiilll.firebaseio.com` / `i----i.firebaseio.com`). This is a one-way, undocumented exfiltration of installer-originated log data to an author-controlled destination, activated purely by importing the module — no opt-in API call required. Additionally, index.js installs a global `E` object that, when invoked, PATCHes arbitrary data to the author's Firebase and uploads to the author's Cloudinary account, and embeds third-party API keys (ImgBB, MapQuest, Telegram bot token). The console-hijack behavior alone is sufficient for block: any application that logs request data, errors containing user input, stack traces, or config will silently leak that content to the author's Telegram channel.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
